verses.text: He is brought to the east gate, and 'the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the east,' His voice like many waters, the earth shining. The glory returns. The presence that departed in judgment comes back in mercy.

He is brought to the east gate, and 'the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the east,' His voice like many waters, the earth shining. The glory returns. The presence that departed in judgment comes back in mercy.

He is brought to the east gate, and 'the glory of the God of Israel came from the way of the east,' His voice like many waters, the earth shining. The glory returns. The presence that departed in judgment comes back in mercy.
The glory enters the house by the east gate, and the Spirit brings him in, where 'the glory of the LORD filled the house.' Filled with God. His house is nothing without His indwelling presence.
The glory enters the house by the east gate, and the Spirit brings him in, where 'the glory of the LORD filled the house.' Filled with God. His house is nothing without His indwelling presence.

A voice declares, 'This is the place of my throne... where I will dwell in the midst of... Israel for ever'; let them defile His name no more. God's abiding home. He purposes to dwell with His people unceasingly.

A voice declares, 'This is the place of my throne... where I will dwell in the midst of... Israel for ever'; let them defile His name no more. God's abiding home. He purposes to dwell with His people unceasingly.
By setting their thresholds beside His they defiled His name; now let them put away their sin, and He will dwell among them. Holiness required. God's nearness calls His people to forsake all idols.
By setting their thresholds beside His they defiled His name; now let them put away their sin, and He will dwell among them. Holiness required. God's nearness calls His people to forsake all idols.
He bids show the house to Israel that they may be ashamed; 'the whole limit... shall be most holy.' Shame leads to reverence. Seeing God's holiness humbles the heart that defiled it.
He bids show the house to Israel that they may be ashamed; 'the whole limit... shall be most holy.' Shame leads to reverence. Seeing God's holiness humbles the heart that defiled it.
He bids show the house to Israel that they may be ashamed; 'the whole limit... shall be most holy.' Shame leads to reverence. Seeing God's holiness humbles the heart that defiled it.
The altar is measured — base, settle, horns, and steps facing east. The place of sacrifice. At the centre of restored worship stands the altar of atonement.
The altar is measured — base, settle, horns, and steps facing east. The place of sacrifice. At the centre of restored worship stands the altar of atonement.

The altar is measured — base, settle, horns, and steps facing east. The place of sacrifice. At the centre of restored worship stands the altar of atonement.
The altar is measured — base, settle, horns, and steps facing east. The place of sacrifice. At the centre of restored worship stands the altar of atonement.
These are its ordinances: offer a bullock for a sin offering, and the priest shall take the blood to cleanse the altar. Consecration by blood. Even the altar must be purified before it serves.
These are its ordinances: offer a bullock for a sin offering, and the priest shall take the blood to cleanse the altar. Consecration by blood. Even the altar must be purified before it serves.

These are its ordinances: offer a bullock for a sin offering, and the priest shall take the blood to cleanse the altar. Consecration by blood. Even the altar must be purified before it serves.
On the second day a kid is offered, and the priests cast salt upon the sacrifices. Daily atonement. The seasoning of salt marks the offering as a lasting covenant.
On the second day a kid is offered, and the priests cast salt upon the sacrifices. Daily atonement. The seasoning of salt marks the offering as a lasting covenant.
On the second day a kid is offered, and the priests cast salt upon the sacrifices. Daily atonement. The seasoning of salt marks the offering as a lasting covenant.
Seven days they purge the altar, and from the eighth 'I will accept you, saith the Lord GOD.' Acceptance promised. After cleansing, God receives His people's worship with favour.
